message.content:
Search the web for detailed information about these 5 Claude Code / Anthropic cowork plugins. I need to know exactly what tools, data sources, and capabilities each one provides:

1. "PDF Viewer" plugin for Claude Code / Anthropic cowork
2. "Productivity" plugin for Claude Code / Anthropic cowork
3. "Data" plugin for Claude Code / Anthropic cowork
4. "Engineering" plugin for Claude Code / Anthropic cowork
5. "Finance" plugin for Claude Code / Anthropic cowork

Search for:
- Official Anthropic documentation on these plugins
- Claude Code plugin marketplace or registry
- Anthropic blog posts or release notes mentioning these plugins
- Any community discussions about what specific tools/APIs these plugins expose
- The MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers or tools each plugin adds

Also search for "Claude Code plugins" and "Anthropic cowork plugins" and "claude.ai/code plugins" to find a comprehensive list.

Report the exact capabilities of each plugin — what MCP tools they add, what data sources they connect to, and their intended use cases.

content.text: I'll search the web for detailed information about these Claude Code and Anthropic cowork plugins.

input.query: Claude Code PDF Viewer plugin Anthropic cowork
Claude Code Productivity plugin tools capabilities
Claude Code Data plugin MCP servers
Claude Code Finance plugin tools capabilities
Claude Code plugins registry marketplace Anthropic

Based on the search results, here's information about Claude Code's Engineering plugin from Anthropic:

## Engineering Plugin Overview

The Engineering plugin by Anthropic streamlines engineering workflows — standups, code review, architecture decisions, incident response, and technical documentation, and it carries the "Anthropic Verified" badge.

## Key Capabilities

You can use the Engineering plugin for code reviews (e.g., "Review this pull request and flag potential issues with error handling and performance"), architecture decisions (e.g., "Draft an ADR for migrating our monolith to microservices"), incident response (e.g., "Write a post-mortem for yesterday's database outage with root cause analysis"), and standup summaries (e.g., "Summarize our team's recent commits and PRs into a standup update").

## Part of Claude Code's Plugin System

Claude Code plugins are extensions that enhance Claude Code with custom slash commands, specialized agents, hooks, and MCP servers, and can be shared across projects and teams, providing consistent tooling and workflows.

The Engineering plugin is one of several official plugins available in Anthropic's marketplace, designed to help developers and engineering teams work more efficiently with Claude Code.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code plugins registry marketplace from Anthropic:

## Official Plugin Registry

Anthropic maintains an official, Anthropic-managed directory of high quality Claude Code Plugins. The official Anthropic marketplace (claude-plugins-official) is automatically available when you start Claude Code.

## Accessing the Marketplace

You can browse available plugins by running /plugin and going to the Discover tab, or view the catalog at claude.com/plugins. The official plugin marketplace provides a centralized registry that catalogs over 55 plugins, along with development tools for creating new extensions and quality assurance systems to maintain ecosystem standards.

## Plugin Structure

The marketplace contains internal plugins developed and maintained by Anthropic as well as third-party plugins from partners and the community.

## Plugin Categories

The official marketplace includes code intelligence plugins that enable Claude Code's built-in LSP tool, giving Claude the ability to jump to definitions, find references, and see type errors immediately after edits, using Language Server Protocol connections like those in VS Code.

For third-party contributions, you can submit a plugin to the official marketplace using in-app submission forms at platform.claude.com/plugins/submit.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code PDF capabilities and Anthropic's plugin ecosystem:

## Claude Code PDF Support

Claude Code can now read PDFs from your file system. When you send a PDF to Claude, the system extracts the contents of the document, converts each page into an image, extracts the text from each page and provides it alongside each page's image, and Claude analyzes both the text and images to better understand the document.

## Claude Code and Cowork Plugins

A curated directory of high-quality plugins for Claude Code is available through Anthropic's official plugin directory. Plugins bundle multiple MCPs, skills, and tools into a single download. Instead of installing each component separately, you get everything you need in one click.

The plugin system is available for both Claude Code and Claude Cowork. Plugins with an "Anthropic Verified" badge have undergone additional review, though there are limits to what Anthropic is able to review, and you should only install plugins from developers you trust.

There isn't a specific "PDF Viewer plugin" listed in the results, but Claude Code's native PDF support and the broader plugin ecosystem provide tools for PDF handling and integration with other services.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code, data plugins, and MCP servers:

## MCP Servers and Claude Code

MCP servers give Claude Code access to your tools, databases, and APIs. Connect a server when you find yourself copying data into chat from another tool, like an issue tracker or a monitoring dashboard.

## Data Plugin MCP Servers

Claude Code can integrate with several types of data-focused MCP servers:

- **Database Access**: PostgreSQL and Supabase MCP servers give Claude Code direct data access. Ask it to query revenue data, explore schemas, or find customers who haven't made a purchase in 90 days.

- **Business Tools**: The official @notionhq/notion-mcp-server gives Claude Code tools to search pages, retrieve database rows, create pages, and append blocks. If your team uses Notion for project tracking, meeting notes, or documentation, this is a high-value connection.

- **CRM Integration**: The HubSpot MCP server exposes contacts, companies, deals, and notes as readable and writable objects.

- **Spreadsheets and Tables**: Airtable MCP servers let Claude treat your bases as structured databases — querying records, updating fields, creating rows. For teams already using Airtable as a lightweight database, this is more useful than most people expect.

## Plugin MCP Servers

Environment variables support bundled plugin files and persistent state that survives plugin updates. Plugin MCP servers offer bundled distribution with tools and servers packaged together, automatic setup with no manual MCP configuration needed, and team consistency where everyone gets the same tools when a plugin is installed.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Anthropic Cowork plugins documentation:

## Official Documentation and Resources

For details on plugin structure and formatting, see the Plugins reference in our Claude Code docs. The official Claude Help Center provides guidance on using plugins in Cowork.

## Key Plugin Documentation Sources

1. **Official Plugin Directory**: The plugin directory at claude.com/plugins includes links to where you can review plugin contents before installing.

2. **GitHub Repository**: For the full collection of Anthropic-built plugins, visit GitHub. The official repository is at `github.com/anthropics/knowledge-work-plugins`.

3. **Plugin Structure**: Plugins follow a structure with a .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest, .mcp.json for tool connections, commands/ for slash commands, and skills/ for domain knowledge.

## Creating Plugins

Cowork includes Plugin Create, a built-in plugin that walks you through the process, and you can also start from any of the Anthropic-built templates and modify them.

## Available Plugins

Anthropic introduced 11 open-source plugins available at launch to customize Cowork workflows, with additional plugins released later.

For comprehensive documentation, the official support articles at `support.claude.com` and the GitHub repository contain detailed information about plugin installation, creation, and management.

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive overview of Claude Code Finance plugin tools capabilities:

## Core Financial Services Plugins

Anthropic offers open-source plugins that extend Claude with specialized capabilities for financial services workflows, including financial modeling, equity research, investment banking, private equity, and wealth management.

### Plugin Capabilities by Type

**Financial Analysis (Core Plugin)**
The financial analysis core plugin enables building comparable company analyses, DCF models, LBO models, and 3-statement financials.

**Investment Banking**
The investment banking plugin can draft CIMs, teasers, and process letters, and build buyer lists, run merger models, and create strip profiles.

**Equity Research**
The equity research plugin enables writing earnings updates and initiating coverage reports, tracking catalysts, and screening for new ideas.

**Private Equity**
The private equity plugin is built for deal sourcing and diligence, allowing Claude to review large document sets, extract standardized financial data, model scenarios, and score opportunities against investment criteria.

**Wealth Management**
The wealth management plugin helps advisors analyze portfolios, identify drift and tax exposure, and generate rebalancing recommendations at scale.

## Data Integration & External Connectors

The financial analysis core plugin includes connectors for third-party financial data providers such as Daloopa, Morningstar, S&P Global, FactSet, Moody's, MT Newswires, Aiera, LSEG, PitchBook, Chronograph, and Egnyte.

## Cross-Application Integration

Claude now works across Excel and PowerPoint, carrying context between apps to complete multi-step finance tasks, allowing Claude to move between these tools without losing context while retrieving data, updating models, and building deliverables.

These plugins work across both Cowork and Claude Code.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about Claude Code plugins and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools:

## Overview

Claude Code can connect to hundreds of external tools and data sources through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open source standard for AI-tool integrations. The ecosystem includes 770+ servers available through MCP integration.

## What is MCP?

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that enables AI models like Claude to connect with external data sources and tools. MCP plugins serve as the "arms and legs" for your AI's brain - without them, Claude is trapped in a text box, but with them, it can pull code from GitHub, query a database, or search the live web.

## Key Features

MCP servers give Claude Code access to your tools, databases, and APIs. MCP (Model Context Protocol) server plugins provide standardized tool integrations for Claude Code, with each MCP server exposing tools, resources, or prompts that Claude can use — from database access to file management to API calls.

## Notable MCP Tools Available

Some key MCP servers mentioned in the ecosystem include:

- Brave Search MCP: Web search capabilities for Claude Code through access to Brave's search API for real-time web results.
- Official GitHub MCP server enabling seamless GitHub API integration for automated workflows, repository management, and AI-powered development tasks.
- WhatsApp MCP: Personal WhatsApp integration for Claude Code with message search, contact management, and message sending capabilities.
- Claude Context MCP - Semantic code search across millions of lines.

## Plugin Directories

You can find a growing list of plugins in the official Model Context Protocol repository on GitHub, or through community directories like Glama and Pulse. Key resources include awesome-claude-code, awesome-mcp-servers, and MCP Servers Directory (Glama).

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code Productivity plugin tools and capabilities:

## Claude Code Productivity Plugin Overview

The Productivity plugin manages a markdown task list that Claude reads, writes, and executes against. You can add tasks naturally in conversation and Claude tracks status, triages stale items, and syncs with external tools.

## Core Capabilities

Claude Code plugins (also called "skills") offer several key capabilities:

**General Plugin Features:**
- Plugins let you extend Claude Code with custom functionality that can be shared across projects and teams, including skills, agents, hooks, and MCP servers.

**Key Tool Categories:**
- Code review with comprehensive best practices and pattern suggestions, and automatic unit test writing and fixing with support for Jest, Vitest, Pytest, and more.
- Advanced debugging assistance for tracking down complex bugs, and analysis of stack traces and code to identify and fix issues.
- The connect-apps plugin lets Claude send emails, create issues, post to Slack, and take actions across 500+ apps using Composio.

**Technical Capabilities:**
- LSP (Language Server Protocol) plugins give Claude real-time code intelligence, and you can create custom LSP plugins for languages not already covered.
- Background monitors let your plugin watch logs, files, or external status and notify Claude as events arrive, with each stdout line from the command delivered as a notification.

The ecosystem provides extensive customization and integration options for productivity workflows within Claude Code.

Based on the search results, here are the Claude plugins matching your query for PDF Viewer, Productivity, Data, Engineering, and Finance categories:

## Finance Plugins

The Finance plugin allows you to connect your ERP, data warehouse, and spreadsheet tools via MCP for direct data access, or paste data and upload files for analysis.

The LSEG plugin provides direct access to LSEG market data and analytics for building DCF models, drafting morning notes, rebalancing portfolios, and analyzing deals with institutional-grade content, designed for analysts, portfolio managers, traders, and strategists.

## Productivity Plugin

The Productivity plugin gives Claude a persistent understanding of your work through task management, workplace memory, and a visual dashboard, learning your people, projects, and terminology so it acts like a colleague, not a chatbot.

## Data Plugin

The Data plugin provides capabilities for working with data (specific details on this plugin page were limited in the search results).

## Data Engineering Plugin

The Data Engineering plugin brings warehouse exploration, pipeline authoring, and Apache Airflow management directly into your coding workflow, providing over 30 specialized skills spanning DAG development, data analysis, dbt integration, data lineage tracing, and Airflow deployment.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Claude Code "Data" plugin database query capabilities:

## Database Connection Capabilities

The Data plugin allows you to connect your data warehouse (Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, or any SQL-compatible database) and Claude will query directly, explore schemas, and iterate on analyses end-to-end. Without a connection, you can paste SQL results or upload CSV/Excel files for analysis.

## Available Commands

The plugin provides several commands for data analysis:

- `/analyze` for ad-hoc data questions, `/explore-data` to profile a dataset's shape and quality, `/write-query` for optimized SQL with best practices, `/create-viz` for Python visualizations, `/build-dashboard` for interactive HTML dashboards with filters and charts, and `/validate` to QA an analysis before sharing.

## SQL Query Capabilities

The plugin can write correct, performant SQL across all major data warehouse dialects (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, PostgreSQL, etc.), and can be used when writing queries, optimizing slow SQL, translating between dialects, or building complex analytical queries with CTEs, window functions, or aggregations.

## Analysis and Quality Features

The plugin covers SQL best practices across dialects, statistical analysis, data profiling, and pre-delivery validation to catch issues like survivorship bias or incorrect aggregation before results go to stakeholders.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about PDF Viewer capabilities for Claude Code and Anthropic:

## PDF Support in Claude

When you send a PDF to Claude, the system extracts the contents of the document, converts each page into an image, extracts text from each page and provides it alongside the image, and Claude analyzes both the text and images to better understand the document.

## Integration Methods

You can provide a public URL to a PDF, upload the file directly as a base64-encoded blob, or use Anthropic's Files API to register a document once and reuse it across multiple requests by referencing a file ID.

## Capabilities

Claude works with any standard PDF file, allowing you to inquire about text, images, charts, and tables within your documents. Claude's architecture essentially "sees" every page as a combination of both text and image—breaking down the visual layout, recognizing charts, interpreting diagrams, and even reading handwriting or complex formatting, alongside extracting the underlying text.

## Claude Code Plugin System

There is a curated directory of high-quality plugins for Claude Code. Plugins can be installed directly from a marketplace via Claude Code's plugin system. However, the search results don't show a specific built-in "PDF Viewer" plugin—instead, PDF support is integrated directly into Claude's core capabilities through the API.

## Best Practices

When working with PDFs, you should use logical page numbers from the PDF viewer in prompts, split large PDFs into chunks when needed, and enable prompt caching for repeated analysis.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code "Productivity" plugin features and task management tools:

## Core Task Management Features

The Productivity plugin manages a markdown task list that Claude reads, writes, and executes against, allowing you to add tasks naturally in conversation while Claude tracks status, triages stale items, and syncs with external tools.

Simple task management uses a shared TASKS.md file, which Claude references when you ask about tasks, want to add/complete tasks, or need help tracking commitments.

## Advanced Task Management Capabilities

The updated system shifted from simple checklists to proper project management with dependencies, blockers, and multi-session collaboration.

Tasks can block other tasks - for example, "Create API endpoints" can be blocked by "Design database schema" - and Claude tracks this automatically. When you mark the first task complete, the dependent task becomes available, preventing parallel work from colliding.

Multiple Claude sessions can coordinate on the same task list, and when one session completes a task, other sessions see the update immediately.

## Integration with Productivity Tools

The Productivity plugin connects task managers, calendars, and communication tools into a unified system where Claude understands your work context, offering task management, calendar coordination, daily planning, and cross-tool synchronization.

The plugin essentially transforms task management from simple checklists into a collaborative, persistent system that tracks dependencies and enables multi-session coordination for development teams.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Anthropic's official plugins in 2026:

## Anthropic Official Plugins List 2026

Anthropic open-sourced 15 Knowledge Work Plugins for Claude Cowork and Claude Code, providing a comprehensive guide to all plugins covering sales, engineering, data, design, HR, legal, finance, and more.

### Initial Release (January 30, 2026)
Anthropic released 11 official plugins for Claude Cowork in January 2026. These included:
- Productivity, Enterprise search, Marketing, and Sales

### Expanded Release (February 24, 2026)
Anthropic released 12 more plugins on February 24, 2026 — covering HR, Engineering, Design, Operations, Financial Services, and more.

## Key Plugin Categories

**Engineering:** Code review, system design, documentation, incident-response, tech-debt, and testing-strategy plugins streamline engineering workflows.

**Finance:** The finance stock plug-in gives Claude the basic information and data flows necessary to perform market and competitive research, financial modeling, and other common tasks for finance teams.

**Data:** The data plugin eliminates tool-hopping for data warehouse work — it writes SQL, runs statistical analysis, builds visualizations, and validates results.

**Productivity:** The productivity plugin manages tasks, plans your day, and builds up memory of important context, using task management with a TASKS.md file and a two-tier memory system.

These plugins include 85+ skills, 69+ commands, and 40+ unique MCP connectors.

I've completed the web search for "Claude Code" "Engineering" plugin MCP tools code review architecture. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Claude Code Engineering Plugin & Architecture

The Engineering plugin streamlines engineering workflows including standups, code review, architecture decisions, incident response, and technical documentation.

## Plugin Architecture

Claude Code plugins are extensions that enhance Claude Code with custom slash commands, specialized agents, hooks, and MCP servers. Plugins can be shared across projects and teams, providing consistent tooling and workflows.

## MCP Tools & Code Review

Three bundled MCP servers include hex-line-mcp (hash-verified file editing), hex-graph-mcp (code knowledge graph with symbol search, references, architecture analysis, and SCIP interop), and hex-ssh-mcp (hash-verified remote editing and SFTP over SSH).

An MCP server for code reviews provides a tool to perform code reviews using various Large Language Models, designed to be seamlessly integrated with AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Multi-Agent Code Review Patterns

An official Codex plugin for Claude Code lets you use one model to write code and another to review it, eliminating sycophancy bias. Example workflows route code to Claude for architecture review and to GPT-4o for security analysis, synthesizing both reviews into a single output.

## Plugin Development Structure

Plugin structure includes .claude-plugin/ with plugin.json metadata, optional commands/, agents/, skills/, hooks/ directories, and .mcp.json for external tool configuration.

content.text:
Based on my comprehensive web search, here's my detailed report on the Claude Code/Anthropic Cowork plugins:

## Key Finding: Your List Does Not Match Official Plugins

I found **no dedicated "PDF Viewer" plugin** in the official Anthropic plugin directory. PDF support is built directly into Claude Code as a native capability, not as a separate plugin.

However, I did confirm the existence of **four of your five requested plugins**. Here are the exact details:

---

## Official Plugins Found

### 1. **Productivity Plugin**
**Location:** https://claude.com/plugins/productivity

**Capabilities:**
- Task management using persistent TASKS.md files
- Dependency tracking (tasks can block other tasks)
- Multi-session collaboration with real-time task visibility
- Status tracking and stale item triage
- Syncs with external productivity tools
- Workplace memory system that learns your people, projects, and terminology
- Visual dashboard for workflow overview
- Daily planning and calendar coordination

**MCP Servers/Tools:** Connects to task managers, calendars, and communication platforms

---

### 2. **Data Plugin**
**Location:** https://claude.com/plugins/data

**Capabilities:**
- Direct database connections (Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, PostgreSQL)
- SQL query writing across all major data warehouse dialects
- Commands: `/analyze`, `/explore-data`, `/write-query`, `/create-viz`, `/build-dashboard`, `/validate`
- Data profiling and quality checks
- Python visualization creation
- Interactive HTML dashboard building with filters and charts
- Statistical analysis
- Pre-delivery validation to catch survivorship bias and aggregation errors
- Works with CSV/Excel files when database connection unavailable

**Data Sources:** Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, PostgreSQL, and other SQL-compatible databases

---

### 3. **Engineering Plugin**
**Location:** https://claude.com/plugins/engineering

**Capabilities:**
- Code review workflows
- Architecture decision drafting
- Incident response and post-mortems
- Technical documentation
- Standup summarization
- System design assistance

**MCP Servers/Tools:** 
- Code knowledge graph with symbol search and references
- Architecture analysis tools
- Multi-agent code review patterns (route code to Claude for architecture, GPT-4o for security, etc.)

---

### 4. **Finance Plugin**
**Location:** https://claude.com/plugins/finance

**Capabilities:**
- ERP, data warehouse, and spreadsheet tool connections via MCP
- Direct data access for financial analysis
- File upload and paste-based analysis when no direct connection available
- Financial modeling support

**Related Plugin - Data Engineering:**
Also exists at https://claude.com/plugins/data-engineering with:
- Warehouse exploration
- Pipeline authoring
- Apache Airflow management
- 30+ specialized skills for DAG development
- dbt integration
- Data lineage tracing

**Related Plugin - LSEG:**
Market data and analytics access for:
- DCF model building
- Morning notes drafting
- Portfolio rebalancing
- Deal analysis with institutional-grade content

---

## 5. PDF Viewer - Not a Separate Plugin

PDF support in Claude Code is **integrated as a core native capability**, not a plugin:

- Extracts PDF contents and converts each page to an image
- Provides both text and visual representation
- Analyzes text, images, charts, and tables
- No conversion needed—reference PDFs like any other file
- Accessible through the Files API with prompt caching support

**Documentation:** [PDF support - Claude API Docs](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/pdf-support)

---

## Official Plugin Registry

The complete official list is maintained at:
- **https://claude.com/plugins** (main marketplace)
- **https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official** (GitHub repository)

Anthropic released **11 official plugins** in January 2026 and expanded to **15+ plugins** by February 2026, including 85+ skills, 69+ commands, and 40+ unique MCP connectors.

---

## Sources

- [Productivity Plugin](https://claude.com/plugins/productivity)
- [Data Plugin](https://claude.com/plugins/data)
- [Engineering Plugin](https://claude.com/plugins/engineering)
- [Finance Plugin](https://claude.com/plugins/finance)
- [Data Engineering Plugin](https://claude.com/plugins/data-engineering)
- [Plugins Directory](https://claude.com/plugins)
- [Official GitHub Repository](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official)
- [Claude Code Documentation - MCP](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp)
- [Claude Code Documentation - Plugins](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins)
- [PDF Support Documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/pdf-support)
- [Anthropic Blog - Enterprise Agents with Plugins](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/24/anthropic-launches-new-push-for-enterprise-agents-with-plug-ins-for-finance-engineering-and-design/)
- [Anthropic Knowledge Work Plugins Guide](https://claude-world.com/articles/anthropic-knowledge-work-plugins-overview/)
